paper_title: Jais and Jais-chat: Arabic-Centric Foundation and Instruction-Tuned Open Generative Large Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces Jais, a 13B-parameter Arabic-centric decoder-only LLM trained on a large-scale multilingual corpus (395B tokens total) with 33% Arabic representation, achieving superior Arabic fluency while maintaining competitive English performance. Addresses the scarcity of high-quality Arabic data through bilingual pretraining and extensive data augmentation techniques.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Mixture of Arabic (72B tokens, upsampled to 116B effective), English (232B tokens), and programming code (46B tokens)
raw_extraction.application: Arabic and English natural language understanding, dialogue systems, reasoning tasks, code generation, and cross-lingual applications requiring deep domain-specific knowledge in Arabic contexts.
raw_extraction.license: Open source (specific license details not explicitly stated in the abstract but implied as open due to release notes)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Developing high-capability Arabic-centric LLMs with strong reasoning and cultural awareness, overcoming limited Arabic data availability and bridging the gap between monolingual English-focused models and low-resource languages like Arabic.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Instruction tuning with Arabic/English bilingual safety-aligned prompts + reinforcement learning via external hate-speech classifiers
raw_extraction.hardware_used: Condor Galaxy Supercomputer, customized version of the Cerebras model zoo
raw_extraction.extension: Uses ALiBi positional encodings for longer context extrapolation, SwiGLU activation functions, maximal update parametrization for hyperparameter optimization, and a custom tokenizer weighing both Arabic and English equally.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Jais and Jais-chat: Arabic-Centric Foundation and Instruction-Tuned Open Generative Large Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Instruction-tuned variant of Jais optimized for conversational interaction, incorporating safety alignment via prompt-based guardrails, keyword filtering, and external classifiers during deployment. Designed for interactive dialogue systems with enhanced adherence to ethical guidelines.
raw_extraction.application: Interactive chat interfaces, dialogue systems, Arabic/English multilingual Q&A, and safety-critical conversational applications.
raw_extraction.research_problem: Enabling safe, aligned, and fluent conversational agents in Arabic and English, ensuring responsible deployment of LLMs in real-world applications.
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Instruction tuning with over 3.6 million Arabic and 6 million English instruction-response pairs, including safety-aligned fine-tuning.
raw_extraction.extension: Real-time **hate-speech detection** via fine-tuned classifiers on JABER (94.8% accuracy) and **keyword-based refusal** using regex patterns from LDNOOBW.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Jais and Jais-chat: Arabic-Centric Foundation and Instruction-Tuned Open Generative Large Language Models

raw_extraction.organization: Canonical Name Not Explicitly Stated (Research Team)
raw_extraction.innovation: Arabic-centric foundation model with instruction-tuning tailored for Arabic language challenges, including exclusion of problematic translations and addition of native Arabic datasets
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale Arabic-centric text corpus (including translated English datasets like Supernatural Instructions, Unnatural, NaturalQuestions, Alpaca, HC3, Dolly-15k, Baize, Basic-Conv, Bactrian, xP3, AraNER, NativeQA-Ar, SafetyQA-Ar)
raw_extraction.application: Arabic language understanding, instruction-following, chat applications, knowledge-based Q&A, commonsense reasoning, and Arabic text generation
raw_extraction.license: Closed Source (No explicit license mentioned, but described as 'open generative' with unspecified distribution terms)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Development of Arabic-focused large language models capable of handling instruction-following and multilingual reasoning while mitigating biases and misinformation risks
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Instruction-tuning with autoregressive objective masking prompt loss only

raw_extraction.organization: Canonical Name Not Explicitly Stated (Research Team)
raw_extraction.innovation: Instruction-tuned variant of Jais 1.3B with built-in safety mechanisms addressing discrimination, toxicity, privacy, and sensitive topic avoidance, leveraging Arabic-specific datasets and translated English safety guidelines
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Same as Jais 1.3B (large-scale Arabic-centric text corpus)
raw_extraction.application: Arabic chat applications, instruction-following, and interactive dialogue systems with safety guardrails
raw_extraction.license: Closed Source (No explicit license mentioned, but described as 'open generative' with unspecified distribution terms)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Instruction-following and safe interaction capabilities for Arabic LLMs, particularly in politically and religiously sensitive contexts
